title: CORDYCEPS
description: Cordyceps is a genus of parasitic fungi used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries. Modern research shows it may enhance athletic performance, oxygen utilization, and provide anti-aging and immune-modulating benefits.

research.title: Cordycepin for Health and Wellbeing: A Potent Bioactive Metabolite of an Entomopathogenic Medicinal Fungus Cordyceps with Its Nutraceutical and Therapeutic Potential
research.summary: Cordycepin, a bioactive component of Cordyceps mushroom, has significant nutraceutical and therapeutic potential for maintaining good health and promoting overall well being.

research.title: The genus Cordyceps: a chemical and pharmacological review
research.summary: Cordyceps species, particularly C. sinensis and C. militaris, demonstrate potential as chemotherapeutic agents due to their chemistry and pharmacological activity.

research.title: Bidirectional regulatory effects of Cordyceps on arrhythmia: Clinical evaluations and network pharmacology
research.summary: Cordyceps exhibits positive effects on treating arrhythmia through regulation of adrenergic signaling in cardiomyocytes and the PI3K-Akt signaling pathway.

research.title: Effect of Cordyceps spp. and Cordycepin on Functions of Bones and Teeth and Related Processes
research.summary: Cordyceps species and cordycepin show potential in developing regenerative medicines for bone and tooth health, expanding treatment options for osteoporosis.

research.title: Cordyceps spp.: A Review on Its Immune-Stimulatory and Other Biological Potentials
research.summary: Cordyceps species demonstrate potential in immune stimulation, nitric oxide production, and other pharmacological activities.

research.title: Efficacy of Cordyceps sinensis as an adjunctive treatment in kidney transplant patients
research.summary: Cordyceps as an adjuvant to routine immunosuppressant therapy may benefit kidney transplant patients, though higher-quality evidence is needed.

research.title: Bioactive compounds from Cordyceps and their therapeutic potential
research.summary: Cordyceps species produce over 200 bioactive compounds exhibiting anti-microbial, anti-apoptotic, anti-cancer, anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties.

sentiment.summary: Users report improved endurance, energy, and exercise performance. Effects are subtle but consistent. Quality and species of cordyceps can significantly impact results.

effects.label: Species Matters:
effects.description: Cordyceps militaris shows more consistent results than sinensis.

effects.label: Gradual Benefits:
effects.description: Effects build over weeks of consistent use.

dosage.label: Standardized Extracts:
dosage.description: Look for extracts with guaranteed beta-glucan content.

dosage.label: Pre-Workout:
dosage.description: Can be taken before exercise for performance benefits.

sideEffects.label: Generally Well Tolerated:
sideEffects.description: Few side effects at normal doses.

sideEffects.label: GI Discomfort:
sideEffects.description: Some may experience mild digestive issues.

sideEffects.label: Autoimmune Caution:
sideEffects.description: May stimulate immune system; use caution with autoimmune conditions.

availability.label: Widely Available:
availability.description: Found in health food stores and online.

availability.label: Quality Varies:
availability.description: Choose reputable brands with third-party testing.

availability.label: Cultivated Forms:
availability.description: Cordyceps militaris is more sustainable and affordable.
